notes: Irregular comparative (melhor); placement before noun shifts meaning toward subjective/figurative sense
Placement before noun = 'different/another'; after noun = 'brand new' -- position-dependent meaning
Same form both genders; placement before noun = 'great/important', after = 'large in size'
Functions as adjective or indefinite pronoun depending on context
Also used adverbially to mean 'itself/himself' -- requires context to disambiguate
Before noun = 'a certain (unspecified)'; after noun/with 'estar' = 'correct' -- meaning shifts with position
Also functions as a standalone interjection ('of course!')
Before noun can imply 'longstanding/familiar' rather than literal age
